2605.07699.title: DRIP-R: A Benchmark for Decision-Making and Reasoning Under Real-World Policy Ambiguity in the Retail Domain
2605.07699.paper_summary: LLM-based agents are increasingly deployed for routine but consequential tasks in real-world domains, where their behavior is governed by inherently ambiguous domain policies that admit multiple valid interpretations. Despite the prevalence of such ambiguities in practice, existing agent benchmarks largely assume unambiguous, well-specified policies, leaving a critical evaluation gap. We introduce DRIP-R, a benchmark that systematically exploits real-world retail policy ambiguities to construct scenarios in which no single correct resolution exists. DRIP-R comprises a curated set of policy-ambiguous return scenarios paired with a realistic customer personas, a full-duplex conversational simulation with tool-calling capabilities and a multi-judge evaluation framework covering policy adherence, dialogue quality, behavioral alignment, and resolution quality. Our experiments show that frontier models fundamentally disagree on identical policy-ambiguous scenarios, confirming that ambiguity poses a genuine and systematic challenge to LLM decision-making.
2605.07699.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基礎的代理人越來越多地被部署於現實世界領域中的常規但重要的任務，這些任務的行為受到固有模糊的領域政策的約束，這些政策允許多種有效的解釋。儘管在實踐中這種模糊性普遍存在，但現有的代理基準在很大程度上假設政策是明確且規範良好的，這留下了一個關鍵的評估空白。我們引入了 DRIP-R，一個基準，系統性地利用現實世界零售政策的模糊性來構建沒有單一正確解決方案的情境。DRIP-R 包含一組精心策劃的政策模糊回報情境，並配有現實的顧客角色、一個具有工具調用能力的全雙工對話模擬，以及一個涵蓋政策遵循、對話質量、行為一致性和解決質量的多評審評估框架。我們的實驗顯示，前沿模型在相同的政策模糊情境上根本上存在分歧，這證實了模糊性對 LLM 決策提出了真正且系統性的挑戰。
2605.07699.author: Hsuvas Borkakoty et.al.
2605.07699.authors: Hsuvas Borkakoty, Sebastian Pohl, Cheng Wang, Bei Chen, Yufang Hou
